page: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-96 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an Intel Xeon processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 96 vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 54 MB L3 cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of dedicated RAM, providing a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core processing capabilities, highlighted by high scores in multi-threaded stress-ng and Geekbench tests. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and AVX512_VNNI, which contribute to strong performance in cryptography, software compilation, and ray tracing. Memory performance is a key strength, with high read and write bandwidths for both small cached and large uncached blocks. However, single-core performance remains average across standard tests, and LLM inference speeds for large models are poor, reflecting the lack of dedicated hardware accelerators.

Given its high resource density and dedicated CPU allocation, the n2-standard-96 offers qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized workloads that can leverage its 96 vCPUs and high memory bandwidth without requiring local SSDs or GPU acceleration. It is well-suited for large-scale database operations, high-throughput Redis deployments, static web serving, multi-threaded software compilation, and parallel data processing tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-96 is a general-purpose server featuring 96 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.6 GHz. It contains 48 physical cores with hyperthreading and a 54 MB L3 cache, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and excellent throughput for database operations, Redis, and static web serving. Conversely, single-core performance is average, and large-scale LLM inference is limited. This instance is highly efficient for parallelized workloads, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-96 is a general-purpose server with 96 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU. It delivers strong multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-96 features 96 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
tagline: A high-core general-purpose instance delivering strong multi-threaded performance and high memory bandwidth for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 384 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 2.6 GHz processor
Strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth
Excellent throughput for database operations and Redis deployments
Supports AVX-512 instruction sets for cryptography and compilation
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
